items.title: Event handlers: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Event handler props should call the function immediately so React knows what to do later.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Pass a function to an event prop; invoking it during render performs the action at the wrong time.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Pass a function to an event prop; invoking it during render performs the action at the wrong time.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.title: Event handlers: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Pass handlers rather than calling them during render and preserve native element semantics. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating native button handler passed, not called during render; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.
